per_fact_results.evidence: Alan Turing was born in London, England in 1912.
He studied at King's College, Cambridge and later earned his PhD from Princeton University.
He studied at King's College, Cambridge and later earned his PhD from Princeton University.
During World War II, Turing worked at Bletchley Park, where he played a crucial role in breaking the German Enigma code.
He developed the Bombe, an electromechanical device used to decipher Enigma-encrypted messages.

Marie Curie was born in Warsaw, Poland in 1867.

per_fact_results.fact: (Marie Curie | created | the elements polonium and radium)
per_fact_results.evidence: She discovered the elements polonium and radium.
Curie worked at the University of Paris, where she became the first female professor.

per_fact_results.fact: (Curie | earned | the Nobel Prize in Physics along with Pierre Curie and Henri Becquerel for their work on radioactivity)
per_fact_results.evidence: In 1903, she received the Nobel Prize in Physics along with Pierre Curie and Henri Becquerel for their work on radioactivity.

per_fact_results.evidence: In 1903, she received the Nobel Prize in Physics along with Pierre Curie and Henri Becquerel for their work on radioactivity.
In 1911, she won a second Nobel Prize, this time in Chemistry, for her discovery of radium and polonium.

per_fact_results.fact: (Curie | founded | the Curie Institutes in Paris and Warsaw)
per_fact_results.evidence: She founded the Curie Institutes in Paris and Warsaw, which remain major medical research centers today.
Marie Curie died in 1934 in Passy, France from aplastic anemia, likely caused by her long-term exposure to radiation.
